name: Gitpod API
what_it_does: Provides an API for programmatically creating and managing ephemeral cloud development environments (CDEs) defined as code, enabling agents to spin up fully configured dev workspaces on demand.
use_cases: Provision isolated development environments for each PR or branch in a CI/CD agent workflow without manual setup
Create on-demand sandboxed workspaces for AI coding agents that need a full development toolchain beyond simple code execution
Build developer experience automation that pre-builds workspaces from git repositories so engineers have instant-start environments
Run integration tests in clean, reproducible workspace environments triggered programmatically via the workspace API
Automate environment lifecycle management — create workspace, run tasks, collect outputs, delete — from an orchestration agent
not_for: Simple code snippet execution where a lightweight sandbox like E2B is more appropriate and cost-effective
Workspaces requiring persistent state that must survive beyond the ephemeral session without explicit snapshot management
Environments requiring GPU compute for ML training or inference workloads
best_when: Your agent needs a full-featured, git-integrated development environment with IDE tooling, not just a code execution sandbox.
avoid_when: Your agent only needs to run isolated code snippets or scripts; the overhead of a full workspace IDE environment is unnecessary.

auth.notes: Supports OAuth2 for user-delegated access and Personal Access Tokens (PAT) for agent/service use. OAuth scopes control read/write access to workspaces. PATs are recommended for non-interactive agent workflows.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan includes limited workspace hours per month for open-source projects
pricing.paid_tiers: Individual ~$9/mo, Team plans from $25/user/mo; enterprise self-hosted licensing available; usage billed per workspace-hour
pricing.notes: Gitpod is transitioning to a new platform (Gitpod Flex). The API surface and pricing may differ between legacy Gitpod and Gitpod Flex. Verify current plan structure at time of integration.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent workspace limits per plan; API rate limits not prominently documented; workspace start times range from 10-60s depending on prebuild availability

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC-based API returns status codes and detail strings. REST API wrapper provides HTTP-mapped errors. Documentation for agent-specific error handling patterns is sparse; most docs target human developers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workspace creation is not idempotent; each call creates a new workspace. Agents should list existing workspaces and reconnect to stopped ones rather than always creating new instances.
known_agent_gotchas: Gitpod is actively migrating from the legacy product to Gitpod Flex with a different API surface; documentation and examples may refer to either version — always verify which API version the endpoint uses
Workspace start times of 10-60 seconds make synchronous agent orchestration impractical; agents must implement async polling or webhook-based notification to know when a workspace is ready

Prebuilds dramatically reduce workspace start time but must be explicitly configured in .gitpod.yml; agents that need fast workspace creation must ensure prebuilds are set up for the target repository
PATs have a fixed expiry and must be rotated manually; long-running agents using PAT auth must implement token expiry detection and graceful re-authentication rather than failing silently
The gRPC API requires protobuf tooling to use directly; most agent integrations should use the REST wrapper, but the REST wrapper lags behind gRPC feature availability in some cases

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with scopes provides better access control than flat API keys. Workspaces run in isolated containers. Gitpod environment variables can inject secrets into workspaces — handle with care.
